articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.name: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.name: Messi celeb Arg Iceland_260609 [1296x729]
images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Vinicius Jr. scores in 4-1 win for Brazil in anti-racism game
article.description: Brazil outclassed Guinea 4-1 in a friendly on Saturday as Joelinton, Rodrygo, Eder Militao and Vinicius Jr scored for the five-times world champions who wore an all-black kit in the first half to promote an anti-racism campaign.
article.linkText: Vinicius inspires Brazil to win in anti-racism game

images.name: Vinicius Junior [600x400]
images.caption: Vinicius Junior scored a goal from the penalty spot to round off a 4-1 friendly win for Brazil against Guinea.

peers.name: Vinicius Junior [1296x1296]
peers.caption: Vinicius Junior scored a goal from the penalty spot to round off a 4-1 friendly win for Brazil against Guinea.

peers.name: Vinicius Junior [1296x729]
peers.caption: Vinicius Junior scored a goal from the penalty spot to round off a 4-1 friendly win for Brazil against Guinea.

peers.caption: Vinicius Junior scored a goal from the penalty spot to round off a 4-1 friendly win for Brazil against Guinea.

peers.name: Vinicius Junior [1296x864]
peers.caption: Vinicius Junior scored a goal from the penalty spot to round off a 4-1 friendly win for Brazil against Guinea.

peers.name: Vinicius Junior [1296x518]
peers.caption: Vinicius Junior scored a goal from the penalty spot to round off a 4-1 friendly win for Brazil against Guinea.

article.story:
Brazil eased past Guinea in a 4-1 win in a friendly on Saturday as Joelinton, Rodrygo, Eder Militao and Vinicius Junior scored for the side, who wore an all-black kit in the first half for the first time ever to promote an anti-racism campaign.

It was the first of two friendlies for Brazil against African nations in support of Real Madrid forward Vinicius, who was racially abused in a LaLiga match at Valencia in May, the 10th time incidents involving him have been reported by LaLiga to prosecutors this season.

Another alleged racist incident occurred on Saturday before the friendly. The abuse reportedly happened at the stadium to a friend and adviser of Vinicius, Felipe Silveira, the Brazilian Football Confederation said.

"The fight against racism, a crime that needs to stop around the world, is also why we are here," CBF president Ednaldo Rodrigues said in a statement. "That's also why our national team played the first half of the match in black. And today, once again, another criminal was publicly exposed."

Newcastle United midfielder Joelinton, making his international debut, opened the scoring in the 26th minute with a close-range finish from a rebound.

Rodrygo extended the lead five minutes later with an unstoppable strike after running clear down the right.

Guirassy Serhou took advantage of a distracted Brazilian defence to pull one back for Guinea with a header following a counter-attack but Militao extended Brazil's lead with a towering header and Vinicius completed the rout from the penalty spot four minutes from time.

Brazil play Senegal in Lisbon on Tuesday.

keyEvents.text: Lucas Paquetá (Brazil) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Brazil 1, Guinea 0. Joelinton (Brazil) right footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal    following a set piece situation.
Goal!  Brazil 2, Guinea 0. Rodrygo (Brazil) right footed shot from a difficult angle on the right to the centre of the goal.
Goal!  Brazil 2, Guinea 1. Serhou Guirassy (Guinea) header from very close range to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Issiaga Sylla with a cross.
First Half ends, Brazil 2, Guinea 1.
Second Half begins Brazil 2, Guinea 1.
Goal!  Brazil 3, Guinea 1. Éder Militão (Brazil) header from the centre of the box to the top left corner. Assisted by Lucas Paquetá with a cross   following a corner.
Casemiro (Brazil) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Substitution, Brazil. Bruno Guimarães replaces Joelinton because of an injury.
Substitution, Brazil. Raphael Veiga replaces Lucas Paquetá.
Substitution, Guinea. José Kanté replaces Serhou Guirassy.
Substitution, Guinea. Dembo Sylla replaces Antoine Conté.
Substitution, Guinea. Morlaye Sylla replaces Morgan Guilavogui.
Substitution, Guinea. Yadaly Diaby replaces François Kamano.
Substitution, Guinea. Seydouba Cissé replaces Naby Keïta.
Substitution, Brazil. Malcom replaces Rodrygo.
Substitution, Guinea. Aguibou Camara replaces Ilaix Moriba because of an injury.
Substitution, Brazil. Pedro replaces Richarlison.
Issiaga Sylla (Guinea) is shown the yellow card for a bad foul.
Goal!  Brazil 4, Guinea 1. Vinícius Júnior (Brazil) converts the penalty with a right footed shot to the centre of the goal.
Substitution, Brazil. Vanderson replaces Danilo.
Substitution, Brazil. Rony replaces Vinícius Júnior.
Second Half ends, Brazil 4, Guinea 1.
